You are surveying a candidate "audit pilot pair" in the repo at /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster. A pilot pair = one AsciiDoc spec subdocument plus its implementing bash module(s), which will be audited together under the Allocation Coding Guide (ACG). The audit looks for: (a) "conceptual comments" in bash source — comments carrying design-time knowledge (what a thing IS, why it's shaped so, how it relates to other concepts) rather than operational mechanics; (b) "ornament" in the spec — sentences in normative position that neither constrain an implementer nor are marked as rationale; (c) paraphrase — the same normative idea worded differently in spec vs source comments.

Candidate area: the CONJURE / cloud-build foundry path.
- Specs: Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc, and glance at RBSAV-ark_vouch.adoc and RBSAB-ark_about.adoc
- Sources: Tools/rbk/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, and the rbfca_/rbfcg_/rbfcp_ files under Tools/rbk/ (Foundry Core step-assembly, GAR-REST, plumb)

Report, with search breadth medium:
1. Pair coherence: does RBSAC map cleanly onto an identifiable set of source files, or does the conjure path smear across many modules? Name the best-fit spec↔source pairing.
2. Audit richness: quote 3-5 short specimens of design-time/conceptual comments in the source files (file:line + the comment text, truncated). Quote 2-3 specimens of spec prose that looks like unmarked ornament or restates something the source also states.
3. Sizes: line counts of the spec and the main source files.
4. Churn/heat-overlap: run `git log --oneline --since="2026-05-01" -- <the spec and source files>` and report how many commits touched them and whether commit messages carry the heat tag ₣BH (a heat actively rewriting the lode/inscribe/yoke/reliquary surface). Also check rbfli_/rbfly_/rbld* files are NOT in your candidate set — those are BH's surface.
5. Verdict line: is this a rich, currently-quiet pilot candidate, or hot/contested?

Return a compact structured report. Do not modify anything.

- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

2	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation produces the
5	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
6	artifact for an
7	{rbtga_ark}
8	in
9	{rbtgi_repository}
10	by running mode-aware verification in
11	{gcb_service}.
12	The
13	{rbtr_director}
14	submits a Cloud Build job via `builds.create` (direct submission), which runs as the
15	{rbtr_mason}
16	service account on the airgap private pool (`RBDC_POOL_AIRGAP`, derived from `RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM` with `-airgap` suffix — no public egress).
17	
18	NOTE: Vouch uses `builds.create` rather than trigger dispatch because the
19	{rbtga_hallmark}
20	is known only after the image arrives in GAR.
21	The private pool matches conjure's execution environment.
22	
23	NOTE: The vouch verdict depends on vessel mode.
24	Conjure: DSSE envelope signature verification of GCB-signed provenance.
25	Bind: digest-pin comparison against the upstream reference.
26	Graft: GRAFTED stamp — explicit acknowledgment of no provenance chain.
27	
28	NOTE: Vouch participates in the {rbsk_in_pool_preflight} only when embedded in graft's combined about+vouch Cloud Build job (`zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit`). The standalone vouch submission (`zrbfv_vouch_submit`, the post-image stage of conjure and bind ordain flows) is out of scope per the premise.
29	
30	The
31	{rbtr_director}
32	vouches for an ark:
33	
34	//axhopt_typed_parameter
35	{rbtga_ark}
36	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
37	.. {rbbc_require}
38	vessel provided as first argument
39	.. {rbbc_require}
40	hallmark provided as second argument

44	.. Source vessel configuration from the {rbrv_regime}
45	.. {rbbc_store}
46	`«VESSEL_MODE»` from {rbrv_vessel_mode}
47	
48	//axhos_step
49	. *Authenticate as
50	{rbtr_director}*:
51	.. Execute
52	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
53	.. {rbbc_store}
54	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
55	
56	//axhos_step
57	. *Gate on About Existence*:
58	.. {rbbc_call}
59	Docker Registry API: HEAD manifests for `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/about:«HALLMARK»`
60	.. {rbbc_require}
61	HTTP 200 response -- {rbtgo_ark_about} must complete before vouch
62	.. If `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/vouch:«HALLMARK»` already exists:
63	{rbbc_warn}
64	re-vouch in progress (see idempotency below)
65	
66	//axhos_step
67	. *Submit Vouch Cloud Build*:
68	.. Construct Build resource JSON with `serviceAccount` as
69	{rbtr_mason},
70	substitution namespace `_RBGV_*`
71	.. Pass registry coordinates, vessel mode, and verify method as substitution variables
72	.. {rbbc_submit}
73	`POST «GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL»` with Build resource JSON
74	.. {rbbc_require}
75	HTTP 200 response
76	.. {rbbc_store}
77	`«BUILD_ID»` from response `.metadata.build.id`
78	.. {rbbc_show}
79	Cloud Console URL for build monitoring
80	
81	//axhos_step
82	. *Cloud Build Step 1: Prepare Verification Keys*:
83	.. Image: alpine (from reliquary)
84	.. If conjure mode: write GCB attestor public key to `/workspace/keys/google-hosted-worker.pub`
85	.. If bind or graft mode: no-op (key not needed)
86	
87	//axhos_step
88	. *Cloud Build Step 2: Mode-Aware Verification* (Python 3):
89	.. Image: gcloud (from reliquary)
90	.. Fetch OAuth token from GCE metadata server
91	.. {rbbc_call}
92	Docker Registry API: GET manifests for `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»` to obtain OCI image index
93	.. Filter out attestation manifests before extracting platforms.
94	OCI image indexes may contain non-runnable attestation manifests (SLSA provenance, SBOMs) with platform `unknown/unknown`.
95	Exclude any manifest entry where _both_ `platform.os` and `platform.architecture` are `unknown`.
96	See: https://docs.docker.com/build/metadata/attestations/attestation-storage/[Docker Attestation Storage].
97	.. {rbbc_store}
98	`«PLATFORM_DIGESTS»` as list of `(platform, digest)` pairs from the remaining (non-attestation) manifest entries
99	.. {rbbc_store}
100	platform list to `/workspace/vouch_platforms.txt`
101	.. Branch on `_RBGV_VESSEL_MODE`:
102	+
103	**Conjure branch**:
104	.. For each `(platform, digest)` in `«PLATFORM_DIGESTS»`:
105	... {rbbc_call}
106	`gcloud artifacts docker images describe --show-provenance` to fetch DSSE envelope (provenance occurrences for the image digest)
107	... Extract payload (base64url), signature (base64url), and keyid from the DSSE envelope using Python `json` module
108	... Decode payload and signature via Python `base64.urlsafe_b64decode`
109	... Reconstruct Pre-Authentication Encoding (PAE): `DSSEv1 {payloadType_len} {payloadType} {payload_len} {payload}`
110	... {rbbc_call}
111	`openssl dgst -sha256 -verify «KEY_PEM» -signature «SIG_BIN» «PAE_BIN»` via subprocess, using attestor public key from step 1
112	... {rbbc_require}
113	signature verification succeeds -- proves GCB signed the provenance
114	... Read now-trusted provenance fields via Python `json`: `builder.id`, `buildType`, `invocationId`, subject digest
115	... {rbbc_store}
116	per-platform verification output to `/workspace/verify-«PLATFORM_SUFFIX».json`
117	.. {rbbc_require}
118	all platforms verify successfully
119	.. Compose `vouch_summary.json` via Python `json.dump`: `{hallmark, vessel, vessel_mode: "rbnve_conjure", verify_method: "dsse-envelope", base_images: [...], platforms: [{verifier, builder_id, build_type, subject_digest, keyid, verdict: "pass", platform}, ...]}`
120	.. Record base image provenance: for each slot (1-3), if `_RBGV_IMAGE_«N»` is non-empty, append `{slot, ref, provenance}` to `base_images` array
121	+
122	**Bind branch**:
123	.. {rbbc_call}
124	Docker Registry API: HEAD manifests for `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»`
125	.. {rbbc_store}
126	`«ACTUAL_DIGEST»` from `Docker-Content-Digest` response header
127	.. Extract `«PINNED_DIGEST»` from the `@sha256:...` suffix of `_RBGV_BIND_SOURCE`
128	.. {rbbc_require}
129	`«ACTUAL_DIGEST»` equals `«PINNED_DIGEST»` -- pin integrity verified
130	.. Compose `vouch_summary.json`: `{hallmark, vessel, vessel_mode: "rbnve_bind", verification: {method: "digest-pin", bind_source, actual_digest, pinned_digest, verdict: "DIGEST_PIN_VERIFIED"}}`
131	+
132	**Graft branch**:
133	.. Compose `vouch_summary.json`: `{hallmark, vessel, vessel_mode: "rbnve_graft", verification: {method: "none", graft_source, verdict: "GRAFTED"}}`
134	.. No verification performed -- the GRAFTED verdict explicitly marks the absence of provenance chain
135	
136	//axhos_step
137	. *Cloud Build Step 3: Assemble and Push Vouch Artifact*:
138	.. Image: docker (from reliquary)
139	.. Create buildx builder instance (inspect-or-create pattern, same as conjure)
140	.. Build multi-platform `FROM scratch` container with summary and per-platform verification files as layers
141	.. Platforms: read from `/workspace/vouch_platforms.txt` (written by step 2 for all modes)
142	.. Push to registry at `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/vouch:«HALLMARK»` via `docker buildx build --push --tag ...`
143	.. Hallmark-only lookup by {rbtgo_ark_inspect} is served by subtree navigation — the vouch package lives directly under `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/`, so no cross-vessel aggregator tag is needed
144	.. {rbbc_require}
145	push succeeds
146	
147	//axhos_step
148	. *Wait for Build Completion* (local polling, not a GCB step):
149	.. The Director's workstation polls the builds.get API after submitting steps 1-3 as a single Cloud Build job
150	.. {rbbc_poll}
151	`GET «GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL»/«BUILD_ID»` every 5 seconds
152	.. Continue while status in [`PENDING`, `QUEUED`, `WORKING`]
153	.. {rbbc_fatal}
154	if timeout or final status is not `SUCCESS` -- no partial vouch artifacts are pushed
155	
156	//axhoot_typed_output
157	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
158	. *Display Results*:
159	.. {rbbc_show}
160	vouch complete with hallmark and vouch artifact tag
161	.. {rbbc_show}
162	verdict type: `DSSE_VERIFIED` (conjure), `DIGEST_PIN_VERIFIED` (bind), or `GRAFTED` (graft)
163	
164	//axhoc_completion
165	Successful execution creates a
166	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
167	artifact in the registry at `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/vouch:«HALLMARK»`.
168	The vouch artifact is a multi-platform container (verification results are architecture-independent metadata; multi-platform is for pull ergonomics so arm64 hosts receive no platform mismatch warning).
169	+
170	*DSSE verification* (conjure only): Provenance is fetched via `gcloud artifacts docker images describe --show-provenance` as a DSSE envelope.
171	The verification script (Python 3) decodes base64url payload and signature, reconstructs PAE, and calls `openssl dgst -sha256 -verify` as a subprocess.
172	Attestor public keys are embedded in the step script (sourced from `projects/verified-builder/` KMS); no runtime KMS dependency.
173	The about/vouch steps run on the gcloud builder image and invoke `gcloud` directly (see {rbsk_no_gcloud} exception).
174	No jq, no slsa-verifier — Python 3 standard library + openssl only. Air-gap compatible (Private Google Access reaches Google APIs).
175	+
176	*Re-vouch idempotency*: Re-running vouch re-executes verification and pushes a new vouch artifact, overwriting the previous.
177	Content is presumed deterministic for conjure and bind; graft re-vouch always produces an identical GRAFTED stamp.
178	+
179	*Inspect integration*: Vouch results are displayed by {rbtgo_ark_inspect} in the Vouch Results section, showing per-platform DSSE verification verdicts (conjure), digest-pin confirmation (bind), or GRAFTED acknowledgment (graft).
180	
181	==== Vouch Substitution Variables
182	
183	All `_RBGV_*` substitution variables are passed in the `builds.create` request body.
184	
185	*Registry and identity*::
186	[cols="1,2"]
187	|===
188	| `_RBGV_GAR_HOST` | GAR Docker host (e.g., `us-central1-docker.pkg.dev`). Source: regime constants.
189	| `_RBGV_GAR_PATH` | GAR repository path (`«PROJECT»/«REPOSITORY»`). Source: regime constants.
190	| `_RBGV_VESSEL` | Vessel sigil. Source: loaded vessel configuration.
191	| `_RBGV_HALLMARK` | Full hallmark string. Source: input parameter.
192	| `_RBGV_VESSEL_MODE` | Vessel mode (`rbnve_conjure`, `rbnve_bind`, or `rbnve_graft`). Source: loaded vessel configuration.
193	|===
194	
195	NOTE: Platform list is discovered by step 2 and written to `/workspace/vouch_platforms.txt`, not passed as a substitution variable.
196	Step 3 reads from workspace rather than re-discovering from the registry.
197	
198	*Mode-specific*::
199	[cols="1,2"]
200	|===
201	| `_RBGV_BIND_SOURCE` | Pinned upstream image reference (bind only). Source: {rbrv_bind_image}.
202	| `_RBGV_GRAFT_SOURCE` | Local image reference at push time (graft only). Source: recorded during {rbtgo_ark_graft}.
203	|===
204	
205	*Base image provenance* (conjure only, empty for other modes)::
206	[cols="1,2"]
207	|===
208	| `_RBGV_IMAGE_1` | Resolved base image reference for slot 1 (full GAR ref if anchored, upstream tag if pass-through). Empty if slot unused.
209	| `_RBGV_IMAGE_1_PROVENANCE` | Provenance type for slot 1: `anchored` (GAR-mirrored via enshrine) or `pass-through` (upstream direct). Empty if slot unused.
210	| `_RBGV_IMAGE_2` | Resolved base image reference for slot 2.
211	| `_RBGV_IMAGE_2_PROVENANCE` | Provenance type for slot 2.
212	| `_RBGV_IMAGE_3` | Resolved base image reference for slot 3.
213	| `_RBGV_IMAGE_3_PROVENANCE` | Provenance type for slot 3.
214	|===
215

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_about}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation assembles the
5	{rbtga_ark_about}
6	metadata artifact for an existing
7	{rbtga_ark_image}
8	in
9	{rbtgi_repository}.
10	The about scripts (`rbgja01`–`rbgja04`) are uniform across all three vessel modes and always execute as Cloud Build steps.
11	This procedure describes those steps — not a standalone Cloud Build job.
12	Conjure and bind embed these steps in their primary Cloud Build job alongside image production; graft combines these steps with vouch steps in a single about+vouch Cloud Build job.
13	All jobs run as the
14	{rbtr_mason}
15	service account on the private pool (`RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM`).
16	
17	NOTE: The about steps receive their context via substitution variables (`_RBGA_*`) and workspace state, not via function parameters.
18	For conjure, the hallmark is read from `/workspace` (computed by the preceding build steps); for bind and graft, it comes from the `_RBGA_HALLMARK` substitution variable.
19	See <<rbtgo_about_substitutions>> below for the full variable reference.
20	
21	NOTE: *Universal reliquary precondition*. Every ordain-path vessel — conjure, bind, and graft — must have `RBRV_RELIQUARY` non-empty before about runs. The four steps below execute on reliquary-sourced tool images (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft) regardless of mode; the parent operation's registry preflight enforces this before any Cloud Build submission.
22	
23	NOTE: The {rbsk_in_pool_preflight} is prepended at the entry-point level (`zrbfd_stitch_build_json`, `zrbfd_mirror_submit`, `zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit`); the about-step assembly itself does not inject it.
24	
25	//axhos_step
26	. *Cloud Build Step 1: Discover Platforms* (Python 3):
27	.. Image: gcloud (from reliquary)
28	.. Fetch OAuth token from GCE metadata server
29	.. {rbbc_call}
30	Docker Registry API: GET manifests for `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»` with OCI media type accept headers
31	.. If response is an OCI image index or Docker manifest list:
32	... Filter out attestation manifests before extracting platforms.
33	OCI image indexes may contain non-runnable attestation manifests (SLSA provenance, SBOMs) alongside real platform manifests.
34	Per the BuildKit attestation storage specification, these attestation manifests are deliberately assigned platform `unknown/unknown` (`"architecture": "unknown", "os": "unknown"`) to prevent container runtimes from pulling them as images.
35	They carry media types such as `application/vnd.in-toto+json` and are annotated with `"vnd.docker.reference.type": "attestation-manifest"`.
36	Exclude any manifest entry where _both_ `platform.os` and `platform.architecture` are `unknown`.
37	+
38	See: https://docs.docker.com/build/metadata/attestations/attestation-storage/[Docker Attestation Storage], https://github.com/moby/buildkit/blob/master/docs/attestations/attestation-storage.md[BuildKit Attestation Storage spec].
39	... {rbbc_store}
40	`«PLATFORMS»` as list of platform descriptors from the remaining (non-attestation) manifest entries
41	.. If response is a single OCI/Docker manifest (typical for bind and graft):
42	... {rbbc_call}
43	GET config blob via digest from manifest's `config.digest` field
44	... {rbbc_store}
45	`«PLATFORMS»` as single-element list from config's `os` and `architecture` fields
46	.. {rbbc_store}
47	platform count for subsequent step branching
48	.. After platform discovery, attempt diags artifact extraction (conjure-only):
49	... {rbbc_call}
50	Docker Registry API: HEAD manifests for `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/diags:«HALLMARK»`
51	... If HTTP 200: fetch manifest, iterate layer blobs, extract diagnostic files (`buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json`, `recipe.txt`) to `/workspace`
52	... If HTTP 404 or error: skip gracefully (expected for bind/graft — no diags artifact exists)
53	.. NOTE: The diags artifact is pushed by conjure step 06 (`rbgjb06-push-diags.sh`) and contains build-time diagnostics that cannot be conveyed via Cloud Build substitution variables (Dockerfile content exceeding the ~4KB limit, BuildKit metadata, Docker daemon cache snapshots).
54	
55	//axhos_step
56	. *Cloud Build Step 2: Syft SBOM Generation*:
57	.. Image: docker (from reliquary; runs syft via `docker run` with reliquary syft image)
58	.. Fetch OAuth token via metadata server (syft authenticates as {rbtr_mason})
59	.. For each platform in `«PLATFORMS»`:
60	... Conjure with per-platform tags (multi-platform): {rbbc_call}
61	`syft registry:«GAR_HOST»/«GAR_PATH»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»-«PLATFORM_SUFFIX»`
62	... Conjure single-platform (single manifest, not an index): {rbbc_call}
63	`syft registry:«GAR_HOST»/«GAR_PATH»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»`
64	... Bind/graft (always via per-platform digest): {rbbc_call}
65	`syft registry:«GAR_HOST»/«GAR_PATH»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image@«PLATFORM_DIGEST»` using per-platform digest from step 1's manifest list
66	+
67	NOTE: Bind and graft images always use `@«PLATFORM_DIGEST»` pinning, even for single-platform images.
68	Grafted images may be stored as OCI image indexes containing attestation manifests (see step 1 filtering); scanning the main tag would cause syft to auto-select the Cloud Build worker's native platform (amd64) from the index, failing when the image contains only a different architecture.
69	Digest pinning bypasses index platform resolution entirely.
70	... {rbbc_store}
71	per-platform SBOM to `/workspace/sbom-«LABEL».json` (e.g., `sbom-amd64.json`; LABEL = architecture + variant without separators)
72	.. NOTE: Syft's `registry:` transport reads directly from the OCI registry — no docker CLI or local pull required.
73	
74	//axhos_step
75	. *Cloud Build Step 3: Assemble build_info.json* (Python 3):
76	.. Image: gcloud (from reliquary)
77	.. For each platform in `«PLATFORMS»`:
78	... Construct `build_info.json` with mode-aware fields organized into nested JSON objects:
79	.... Root: `hallmark`, `vessel_mode`, `vessel_name`, `platform`, `image_digest`, `about_timestamp`
80	.... `git` object: `repo`, `branch`, `commit`
81	.... Conjure `build` object: `build_id` (from `_RBGA_BUILD_ID`), `inscribe_timestamp`, `qemu_used`
82	.... Conjure `slsa` object: `build_level`, `build_invocation_id`, `provenance_predicate_types`, `provenance_builder_id`
83	.... Bind `bind` object: `source` (from `_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE`)
84	.... Graft `graft` object: `source` (from `_RBGA_GRAFT_SOURCE`)
85	... {rbbc_store}
86	per-platform `build_info.json` to `/workspace/build_info-«LABEL».json` (e.g., `build_info-amd64.json`)
87	.. Write `recipe.txt` using dual-source logic:
88	... If `recipe.txt` already exists in `/workspace` (written by diags extraction in step 1): skip substitution variable write, log that `recipe.txt` came from the diags artifact
89	... Else if `_RBGA_DOCKERFILE_CONTENT` is non-empty: {rbbc_store}
90	`recipe.txt` to `/workspace/recipe.txt` from substitution variable content
91	... Else: omit `recipe.txt`
92	.. NOTE: The {rbtr_director} reads the Dockerfile locally and passes its content as `_RBGA_DOCKERFILE_CONTENT`.
93	If the file exceeds the Cloud Build substitution variable size limit (~4KB), the director omits it with a warning.
94	The diags path (conjure only) bypasses this limit by conveying the full Dockerfile via a registry artifact.
95	`_RBGA_DOCKERFILE_CONTENT` is retained for all modes as a fallback when diags extraction fails or is absent.
96	
97	//axhos_step
98	. *Cloud Build Step 4: Build and Push About Artifact*:
99	.. Image: docker (from reliquary)
100	.. Create buildx builder instance (inspect-or-create pattern)
101	.. Build multi-platform `FROM scratch` container with per-platform SBOM, `build_info.json`, `recipe.txt` (when present), and conjure enrichment files when forwarded from the diags artifact: `buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json` (conditional COPY — included only if present in `/workspace`)
102	.. For single-platform images: build single-platform about container
103	.. For multi-platform images: build with `buildx --push` using `TARGETARCH`/`TARGETVARIANT` to select per-platform files
104	.. Push to registry tagged `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/about:«HALLMARK»` via `docker buildx build --push`
105	.. {rbbc_require}
106	push succeeds
107	
108	//axhoc_completion
109	Successful execution of these four Cloud Build steps produces a
110	{rbtga_ark_about}
111	artifact in the registry tagged as `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/about:«HALLMARK»`.
112	The about artifact is a multi-platform container matching the image artifact's platform list.
113	+
114	*Re-about idempotency*: Re-running about re-executes Syft and rebuilds `build_info.json`, overwriting the previous about artifact.
115	SBOM content may vary if Syft discovers different packages (e.g., after a Syft version update).
116	+
117	*Implementation note*: About and vouch share common Build resource JSON construction patterns (worker pool, mason SA, registry coordinates).
118	The implementation should extract a shared helper for `builds.create` request assembly.
119	
120	//axhos_waymark
121	[[rbtgo_about_substitutions]]
122	==== About Substitution Variables
123	
124	All `_RBGA_*` substitution variables are passed in the `builds.create` request body.
125	
126	*Registry and identity*::
127	[cols="1,2"]
128	|===
129	| `_RBGA_GAR_HOST` | GAR Docker host. Source: regime constants.
130	| `_RBGA_GAR_PATH` | GAR repository path. Source: regime constants.
131	| `_RBGA_VESSEL` | Vessel sigil. Source: loaded vessel configuration.
132	| `_RBGA_HALLMARK` | Full hallmark string. Source: input parameter.
133	| `_RBGA_VESSEL_MODE` | Vessel mode (`rbnve_conjure`, `rbnve_bind`, or `rbnve_graft`). Source: loaded vessel configuration.
134	| `_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE` | Ark basename for image artifact. Source: `RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE` constant.
135	| `_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT` | Ark basename for about artifact. Source: `RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT` constant.
136	| `_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS` | Ark basename for diags artifact. Source: `RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS` constant.
137	|===
138	
139	*Git metadata*::
140	[cols="1,2"]
141	|===
142	| `_RBGA_GIT_COMMIT` | Main repo HEAD SHA at operation time.
143	| `_RBGA_GIT_BRANCH` | Main repo branch at operation time.
144	| `_RBGA_GIT_REPO` | Main repo identifier (owner/repo).
145	|===
146	
147	*Mode-specific*::
148	[cols="1,2"]
149	|===
150	| `_RBGA_BUILD_ID` | Conjure Cloud Build job ID (conjure only). In the combined conjure job, populated from the built-in `$BUILD_ID`. In manual recovery standalone about, passed by the director from a prior conjure run. Used by step 3 to populate `build_info.json`. Empty for bind/graft.
151	| `_RBGA_INSCRIBE_TIMESTAMP` | Inscribe timestamp from hallmark (conjure only). Empty for bind/graft.
152	| `_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE` | Pinned upstream image reference (bind only). Source: {rbrv_bind_image}. Empty for conjure/graft.
153	| `_RBGA_GRAFT_SOURCE` | Local image reference at push time (graft only). Empty for conjure/bind.
154	| `_RBGA_DOCKERFILE_CONTENT` | Dockerfile content read locally by {rbtr_director} (all modes, optional). Source: {rbrv_conjure_dockerfile} (conjure, always populated), {rbrv_graft_optional_dockerfile} (graft, optional), {rbrv_bind_optional_dockerfile} (bind, optional). Empty if file exceeds ~4KB substitution limit or path not configured. Written to `recipe.txt` in step 3.
155	|===
156	
157	NOTE: `«PLATFORMS»` is discovered in step 1 and passed to subsequent steps as a workspace variable (written to `/workspace/platforms.txt`), not as a substitution variable.

26	# Step Assembly (zrbfc_*)
27	
28	# Internal: Resolve tool image references from reliquary.
29	# Must be called after vessel load (reads RBRV_RELIQUARY).
30	# Sets module-level z_rbfc_tool_* mutable kindle state for downstream step assembly.
31	# Idempotent — safe to call multiple times per invocation.
32	zrbfc_resolve_tool_images() {
33	  zrbfc_sentinel
34	
35	  local -r z_reliquary="${RBRV_RELIQUARY:-}"
36	  test -n "${z_reliquary}" \
37	    || buc_die "RBRV_RELIQUARY is required — run inscribe to create a reliquary first"
38	
39	  local -r z_rqy_prefix="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_reliquary}"
40	  z_rbfc_tool_gcloud="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD}:${z_reliquary}"
41	  z_rbfc_tool_docker="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER}:${z_reliquary}"
42	  z_rbfc_tool_alpine="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE}:${z_reliquary}"
43	  z_rbfc_tool_syft="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT}:${z_reliquary}"
44	  z_rbfc_tool_binfmt="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT}:${z_reliquary}"
45	  z_rbfc_tool_skopeo="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO}:${z_reliquary}"
46	  z_rbfc_tool_gcrane="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCRANE}:${z_reliquary}"
47	  buc_log_args "Tool images resolved from reliquary: ${z_reliquary}"
48	}
49	
50	# Internal: assemble about step scripts into JSON array file
51	# Args: output_file temp_prefix
52	# Reads ZRBFC_RBGJA_STEPS_DIR and z_rbfc_tool_* image refs from module state
53	zrbfc_assemble_about_steps() {
54	  zrbfc_sentinel
55	
56	  local -r z_output_file="$1"
57	  local -r z_temp_prefix="$2"
58	
59	  # Step definitions: script|builder|entrypoint|id
60	  # Delimiter is | because image refs contain colons (sha256 digests)
61	  local -r z_about_step_defs=(
62	    "rbgja01-discover-platforms.py|${z_rbfc_tool_gcloud}|python3|discover-platforms"
63	    "rbgja02-syft-per-platform.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|syft-per-platform"
64	    "rbgja03-build-info-per-platform.py|${z_rbfc_tool_gcloud}|python3|build-info-per-platform"
65	    "rbgja04-assemble-push-about.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|assemble-push-about"
66	  )
67	
68	  echo "[]" > "${z_output_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize about steps JSON"
69	
70	  local z_adef=""
71	  local z_ascript=""
72	  local z_abuilder=""
73	  local z_aentrypoint=""
74	  local z_aid=""
75	  local z_ascript_path=""
76	  local z_abody_file=""
77	  local z_aescaped_file=""
78	  local z_asteps_file=""
79	  local z_abody=""
80	  local z_ashebang=""
81	
82	  for z_adef in "${z_about_step_defs[@]}"; do
83	    IFS='|' read -r z_ascript z_abuilder z_aentrypoint z_aid <<< "${z_adef}"
84	    z_ascript_path="${ZRBFC_RBGJA_STEPS_DIR}/${z_ascript}"
85	    z_abody_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_aid}_body.txt"
86	    z_aescaped_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_aid}_escaped.txt"
87	    z_asteps_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_aid}_steps.json"
88	
89	    test -f "${z_ascript_path}" || buc_die "About step script not found: ${z_ascript_path}"
90	
91	    buc_log_args "Reading script body for ${z_aid} (skip shebang)"
92	    zrbfc_write_script_body "${z_ascript_path}" "${z_abody_file}" \
93	      || buc_die "Failed to read about step script: ${z_ascript_path}"
94	    z_abody=$(<"${z_abody_file}")
95	    test -n "${z_abody}" || buc_die "Empty about script body: ${z_ascript_path}"
96	
97	    buc_log_args "Baking pinned image refs into script text"
98	    z_abody="${z_abody//\$\{ZRBF_TOOL_SYFT\}/${z_rbfc_tool_syft}}"
99	
100	    case "${z_aentrypoint}" in
101	      bash)    z_ashebang="#!/bin/bash" ;;
102	      sh)      z_ashebang="#!/bin/sh" ;;
103	      python3) z_ashebang="#!/usr/bin/env python3" ;;
104	      *)       buc_die "Unknown entrypoint: ${z_aentrypoint}" ;;
105	    esac
106	    printf '%s\n%s' "${z_ashebang}" "${z_abody}" > "${z_aescaped_file}" \
107	      || buc_die "Failed to write about script body for ${z_aid}"
108	
109	    buc_log_args "Appending about step ${z_aid} to JSON array"
110	    jq \
111	      --arg name "${z_abuilder}" \
112	      --arg id "${z_aid}" \
113	      --rawfile script "${z_aescaped_file}" \
114	      '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, script: $script}]' \
115	      "${z_output_file}" > "${z_asteps_file}" \
116	      || buc_die "Failed to append about step ${z_aid} to JSON"
117	    mv "${z_asteps_file}" "${z_output_file}" \
118	      || buc_die "Failed to update about steps JSON for ${z_aid}"
119	  done
120	}
121	
122	# Internal: assemble vouch step scripts into JSON array file
123	# Args: output_file temp_prefix
124	# Reads ZRBFC_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR and z_rbfc_tool_* image refs from module state
125	zrbfc_assemble_vouch_steps() {
126	  zrbfc_sentinel
127	
128	  local -r z_output_file="$1"
129	  local -r z_temp_prefix="$2"
130	
131	  # Step definitions: script|builder|entrypoint|id
132	  # Delimiter is | because image refs contain colons (sha256 digests)
133	  local -r z_vouch_step_defs=(
134	    "rbgjv01-download-verifier.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_alpine}|sh|prepare-keys"
135	    "rbgjv02-verify-provenance.py|${z_rbfc_tool_gcloud}|python3|verify-provenance"
136	    "rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|assemble-push-vouch"
137	  )
138	
139	  echo "[]" > "${z_output_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize vouch steps JSON"
140	
141	  local z_vdef=""
142	  local z_vscript=""
143	  local z_vbuilder=""
144	  local z_ventrypoint=""
145	  local z_vid=""

96	# Verify reliquary tool images exist in GAR.
97	# Full-subtree presence check: every canonical tool image must be present at
98	# the stamp. Inscribe creates all 6 atomically; piecemeal jettison or registry
99	# damage compromises the stamp's integrity. Misses are accumulated across the
100	# whole tool set so the operator sees full damage in one report.
101	# Args: token vessel_dir
102	zrbfd_preflight_reliquary() {
103	  zrbfd_sentinel
104	
105	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
106	  local -r z_vessel_dir="${2:-}"
107	  test -n "${z_token}"      || buc_die "zrbfd_preflight_reliquary: token required"
108	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "zrbfd_preflight_reliquary: vessel_dir required"
109	
110	  local -r z_reliquary="${RBRV_RELIQUARY:-}"
111	  test -n "${z_reliquary}" || buc_die "RBRV_RELIQUARY required on every ordain-path vessel — yoke a reliquary stamp via tt/${RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY}.sh before ordaining"
112	
113	  buc_step "Verifying reliquary tool images exist in GAR"
114	
115	  local -r z_canonical_tools=(
116	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD}"
117	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER}"
118	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE}"
119	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT}"
120	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT}"
121	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO}"
122	  )
123	
124	  local z_missing=()
125	  local z_tool=""
126	  local z_pkg=""
127	  local z_status_file=""
128	  local z_response_file=""
129	  local z_stderr_file=""
130	  local z_http_code=""
131	
132	  for z_tool in "${z_canonical_tools[@]}"; do
133	    z_pkg="${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_reliquary}/${z_tool}"
134	    z_status_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_${z_tool}_status.txt"
135	    z_response_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_${z_tool}_response.txt"
136	    z_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_${z_tool}_stderr.txt"
137	
138	    curl --head -sS \
139	      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
140	      --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
141	      -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
142	      -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
143	      -w "%{http_code}" \
144	      -o "${z_response_file}" \
145	      "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg}/manifests/${z_reliquary}" \
146	      > "${z_status_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
147	      || buc_die "HEAD request failed for reliquary tool: ${z_pkg}:${z_reliquary} — see ${z_stderr_file}"
148	
149	    z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
150	    test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for reliquary check: ${z_tool}"
151	
152	    case "${z_http_code}" in
153	      200) buc_log_args "Reliquary tool present: ${z_tool}" ;;
154	      404) z_missing+=("${z_tool}") ;;
155	      *)   buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_http_code} when checking reliquary tool: ${z_pkg}:${z_reliquary}" ;;
156	    esac
157	  done
158	
159	  if test "${#z_missing[@]}" -eq 0; then
160	    buc_info "Reliquary verified: ${z_reliquary} (${#z_canonical_tools[@]}/${#z_canonical_tools[@]} tools present)"
161	    return 0
162	  fi
163	
164	  buc_warn "Reliquary integrity check failed: ${z_reliquary} (${#z_missing[@]}/${#z_canonical_tools[@]} tools missing)"
165	  buc_bare "  The reliquary is a co-versioned set of builder tool images (gcloud, docker,"
166	  buc_bare "  syft, alpine, binfmt, skopeo) inscribed from upstream into your private GAR."
167	  buc_bare "  Air-gapped worker pools cannot pull from the public internet — the reliquary"
168	  buc_bare "  stages these tools so builds can run without egress. Piecemeal jettison is"
169	  buc_bare "  allowed but unrecoverable surgically: re-inscribe the whole stamp."
170	  buc_bare ""
171	  for z_tool in "${z_missing[@]}"; do
172	    buc_bare "  PRECHECK: GAR image not found at ${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_reliquary}/${z_tool}"
173	    buc_bare "    Required by ${RBRV_SIGIL}'s RBRV_RELIQUARY=${z_reliquary}."
174	  done
175	  buc_bare ""
176	  buc_bare "  Recover by re-inscribing the reliquary, then re-yoking and re-ordaining:"
177	  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY}"
178	  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY}" "<new-stamp>"
179	  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
180	  buc_die "Registry preflight failed — ${#z_missing[@]} of ${#z_canonical_tools[@]} reliquary tool images missing from GAR"
181	}

184	# Check concurrent build quota against regime requirements
185	# Args: token mode
186	#   mode: "gate" (die if insufficient) or "advisory" (warn if insufficient)
187	zrbfd_quota_preflight() {
188	  zrbfd_sentinel
189	
190	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
191	
192	  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbfd_quota_preflight: token required"
193	
194	  # Extract vCPU count from machine type (last segment after final hyphen)
195	  local -r z_vcpus="${RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE##*-}"
196	  case "${z_vcpus}" in
197	    ""|0|*[!0-9]*)
198	      buc_warn "Cannot parse vCPU count from RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE='${RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE}' -- skipping quota preflight"
199	      return 0
200	      ;;
201	  esac
202	
203	  buc_log_args "Machine type ${RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE} = ${z_vcpus} vCPUs"
204	
205	  # Query Service Usage consumer quota API for concurrent_private_pool_build_cpus
206	  local -r z_metric_encoded="cloudbuild.googleapis.com%2Fconcurrent_private_pool_build_cpus"
207	  local -r z_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_SERVICEUSAGE}${RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1BETA1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/services/cloudbuild.googleapis.com/consumerQuotaMetrics/${z_metric_encoded}"
208

210	  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "quota_preflight"
211	
212	  local z_code=""
213	  z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "quota_preflight") || z_code=""
214	  if test "${z_code}" != "200"; then
215	    buc_warn "Could not query build quota (HTTP ${z_code}) -- skipping preflight check"
216	    return 0
217	  fi
218	
219	  # Filter quota response to region-specific bucket via intermediate file
220	  rbuh_jq_file_to_file_ok "quota_preflight" "quota_region" \
221	    "[.consumerQuotaLimits[0].quotaBuckets[] | select(.dimensions.region == \"${RBRD_GCP_REGION}\")] | .[0] // {}" \
222	    || true
223	
224	  # Extract effective limit from region bucket, then fallback to first bucket
225	  local z_limit=""
226	  z_limit=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "quota_region" '.effectiveLimit') || z_limit=""
227	  if test -z "${z_limit}"; then
228	    z_limit=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "quota_preflight" \
229	      '.consumerQuotaLimits[0].quotaBuckets[0].effectiveLimit') || z_limit=""
230	  fi
231	
232	  if test -z "${z_limit}"; then
233	    buc_warn "Could not extract quota limit -- skipping preflight check"
234	    return 0
235	  fi
236	
237	  # -1 means unlimited
238	  if test "${z_limit}" = "-1"; then
239	    buc_info "Quota: unlimited concurrent private pool build CPUs"
240	    return 0
241	  fi
242	
243	  # Compute max concurrent builds
244	  local -r z_max_concurrent=$((z_limit / z_vcpus))
245	
246	  buc_log_args "Quota ${z_limit} vCPUs, machine ${z_vcpus} vCPUs, max concurrent ${z_max_concurrent}, required ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS}"
247	
248	  if test "${z_max_concurrent}" -lt "${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS}"; then
249	    buc_warn "Build quota insufficient: ${z_limit} vCPU quota / ${z_vcpus} vCPUs per build = ${z_max_concurrent} concurrent (need ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS})"
250	    buc_warn "Fresh depots start with a low quota. After some build activity, the Edit Quotas option becomes available."
251	    buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_QUOTA_BUILD}"
252	  else
253	    buc_info "Quota OK: ${z_limit} vCPU / ${z_vcpus} per build = ${z_max_concurrent} concurrent (need ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS})"
254	  fi

257	# Internal: Verify that all required images exist in GAR before submitting
258	# to Cloud Build. Checks two layers in dependency order:
259	#
260	#   1. Reliquary — co-versioned builder tool images (gcloud, docker, syft, etc.)
261	#      created by inscribe. One reliquary per depot setup. Base capture depends on it.
262	#   2. Base images — upstream bases captured into private GAR as bole Lodes via
263	#      ensconce, pinned by content hash. One Lode per base image; the conjure
264	#      ANCHOR resolves to it. Multiple vessels sharing a base reuse one Lode.
265	#
266	# A missing image at either layer causes a Cloud Build failure minutes later.
267	# This preflight catches it immediately with copy-paste remediation commands.
268	#
269	# Must be called after vessel load (reads RBRV_RELIQUARY, RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR)
270	# and authentication (needs token for registry API).
271	zrbfd_registry_preflight() {
272	  zrbfd_sentinel
273	
274	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
275	  local -r z_vessel_dir="${2:-}"
276	  test -n "${z_token}"      || buc_die "zrbfd_registry_preflight: token required"
277	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "zrbfd_registry_preflight: vessel_dir required"
278	
279	  # --- Layer 1: Reliquary tool images ---
280	  zrbfd_preflight_reliquary "${z_token}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
281	
282	  # --- Layer 2: Base images (bole Lodes) ---
283	  # Each vessel declares base images via RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN (upstream tag) and
284	  # RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR (locator: package-path:tag). Ensconce captures the upstream
285	  # image into a bole Lode (rbi_ld), pinned by content hash; the derived-pull
286	  # election populates the ANCHOR. The conjure Dockerfile's FROM references the
287	  # locator-resolved GAR ref, not the upstream tag.
288	
289	  buc_step "Verifying base images exist in GAR"
290	
291	  local z_n=""
292	  local z_anchor_var=""
293	  local z_anchor=""
294	  local z_pkg_path=""
295	  local z_tag=""
296	  local z_origin_var=""
297	  local z_origin=""
298	  local z_any_checked="false"
299	  local z_status_file=""
300	  local z_response_file=""
301	  local z_stderr_file=""
302	  local z_http_code=""
303	
304	  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
305	    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
306	    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ANCHOR"
307	    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
308	    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"
309	
310	    # Skip slots without an origin (no base image to capture).
311	    test -n "${z_origin}" || continue
312	
313	    # Anchor handling depends on egress mode. Tether passes through (buildx
314	    # fetches upstream at build time). Airgap requires a populated anchor
315	    # because the worker pool has no upstream egress — an empty anchor with
316	    # a non-empty origin guarantees runtime build failure.
317	    if test -z "${z_anchor}"; then
318	      if test "${RBRV_EGRESS_MODE:-}" = "rbnve_airgap"; then
319	        # Two anchor-population paths: bole capture (origin is a public upstream)
320	        # and hallmark-pin (origin names a producer vessel in this repo).
321	        # Discriminate by whether origin resolves to a vessel directory.
322	        if test -d "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_origin}"; then
323	          buc_warn "Airgap vessel ${RBRV_SIGIL} has empty ${z_anchor_var}; origin ${z_origin} names a producer vessel"
324	          buc_bare "  ${z_anchor_var} is a hallmark-pin, not a bole locator — ensconce is not invoked on this vessel."
325	          buc_bare "  Ordain the producer vessel first, then write its hallmark into ${z_anchor_var}."
326	          buc_bare "  Canonical handbook path:"
327	          buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ONBOARD_DIR_AIRGAP}"
328	          buc_bare "  Minimal manual sequence:"
329	          buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_origin}"
330	          buc_bare "    export PRODUCER_HALLMARK=\$(cat \${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK})"
331	          buc_bare "    # set ${z_anchor_var}=rbi_hm/\${PRODUCER_HALLMARK}/image:\${PRODUCER_HALLMARK}"
332	          buc_bare "    # in ${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env, then:"
333	          buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
334	          buc_die "Registry preflight failed — airgap vessel missing hallmark-pin anchor"
335	        else
336	          buc_warn "Airgap vessel ${RBRV_SIGIL} has empty ${z_anchor_var} but non-empty ${z_origin_var}=${z_origin}"
337	          buc_bare "  Airgap conjure cannot reach upstream — base images must be captured (ensconced) first."
338	          buc_bare "  The anchor locator points at the captured base Lode inside GAR. Without it,"
339	          buc_bare "  the airgap worker pool has no source for the base image and the build fails."
340	          buc_bare "  Run ensconce, then re-run ordain:"
341	          buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ENSCONCE_BOLE}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
342	          buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
343	          buc_die "Registry preflight failed — airgap vessel missing required anchor"
344	        fi
345	      fi
346	      continue
347	    fi
348	
349	    case "${z_anchor}" in
350	      *:*) : ;;
351	      *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
352	    esac
353	    z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
354	    z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
355	    test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
356	    test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
357	
358	    z_any_checked="true"
359	    z_status_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}base_${z_n}_status.txt"
360	    z_response_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}base_${z_n}_response.txt"
361	    z_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}base_${z_n}_stderr.txt"
362	
363	    curl --head -sS \
364	      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
365	      --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
366	      -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
367	      -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
368	      -w "%{http_code}" \
369	      -o "${z_response_file}" \
370	      "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg_path}/manifests/${z_tag}" \
371	      > "${z_status_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
372	      || buc_die "HEAD request failed for base image: ${z_anchor} — see ${z_stderr_file}"
373	
374	    z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
375	    test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for base image check"
376	
377	    if test "${z_http_code}" = "404"; then
378	      buc_warn "Base image Lode not found: ${z_anchor} (from ${z_origin})"
379	      buc_bare "  Ensconce captures upstream base images (e.g., busybox:latest from Docker Hub) into"
380	      buc_bare "  a bole Lode in your private GAR, pinned by content hash. Like the reliquary, this"
381	      buc_bare "  ensures air-gapped builds never reach the public internet. The anchor locator is"
382	      buc_bare "  stable until you deliberately re-ensconce to pick up a newer upstream version."
383	      buc_bare "  Multiple vessels sharing the same base image reuse one Lode."
384	      buc_bare "  Run ensconce, then re-run ordain:"
385	      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ENSCONCE_BOLE}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
386	      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
387	      buc_die "Registry preflight failed — base image Lode missing from GAR"
388	    elif test "${z_http_code}" != "200"; then
389	      buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_http_code} when checking base image: ${z_anchor}"
390	    fi
391	
392	    buc_log_args "Base image verified: ${z_anchor}"
393	  done
394	
395	  if test "${z_any_checked}" = "true"; then
396	    buc_info "All base images verified in GAR"
397	  fi
398	}

401	zrbfd_stitch_build_json() {
402	  zrbfd_sentinel
403	
404	  local -r z_output_path="${1:?Output path required}"
405	  local -r z_hallmark="${2:?Hallmark required}"
406	  local -r z_context_tag="${3:?Context image tag required}"
407	
408	  buc_log_args "Stitching builds.create JSON to ${z_output_path}"
409	
410	  # Preconditions: vessel loaded and git state captured
411	  test -s "${ZRBFC_VESSEL_SIGIL_FILE}" || buc_die "Vessel not loaded — call zrbfc_load_vessel first"
412	  test -s "${ZRBFC_GIT_INFO_FILE}"     || buc_die "Git info not captured — ensure git metadata is captured before stitch"
413	
414	  buc_log_args 'Read vessel state for substitutions'
415	  local -r z_sigil=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_SIGIL_FILE}")
416	  test -n "${z_sigil}" || buc_die "Empty vessel sigil"
417	  local -r z_dockerfile_name="${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE##*/}"
418	  local -r z_platforms="${RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS// /,}"
419	
420	  # Resolve base images: ANCHOR (locator) → full GAR reference, or pass ORIGIN through.
421	  # Spec: RBSAC step "Resolve Base Images"
422	  # The locator carries its own namespace path (e.g. rbi_ld/<touchmark>:rbi_bole);
423	  # paths within a GAR repo are prefix-free per the wrest/jettison convention.
424	  # Locator captures (z_image_locator_n) feed _RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_n substitutions
425	  # for the in-pool preflight step — anchored slots get HEAD-checked, pass-through
426	  # slots stay empty (preflight cannot reach upstream from the worker pool).
427	  local -r z_gar_repo_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
428	  local z_image_ref_1="" z_image_ref_2="" z_image_ref_3=""
429	  local z_image_locator_1="" z_image_locator_2="" z_image_locator_3=""
430	  local z_ri_n="" z_ri_origin_var="" z_ri_anchor_var="" z_ri_origin="" z_ri_anchor=""
431	  local z_ri_pkg_path=""
432	  local z_ri_tag=""
433	  for z_ri_n in 1 2 3; do
434	    z_ri_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_ri_n}_ORIGIN"
435	    z_ri_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_ri_n}_ANCHOR"
436	    z_ri_origin="${!z_ri_origin_var:-}"
437	    z_ri_anchor="${!z_ri_anchor_var:-}"
438	    test -n "${z_ri_origin}" || continue
439	    local z_ri_ref=""
440	    local z_ri_locator=""
441	    if test -n "${z_ri_anchor}"; then
442	      case "${z_ri_anchor}" in
443	        *:*) : ;;
444	        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_ri_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_ri_anchor}" ;;
445	      esac
446	      z_ri_pkg_path="${z_ri_anchor%:*}"
447	      z_ri_tag="${z_ri_anchor##*:}"
448	      test -n "${z_ri_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_ri_anchor_var}: ${z_ri_anchor}"
449	      test -n "${z_ri_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_ri_anchor_var}: ${z_ri_anchor}"
450	      z_ri_ref="${z_gar_repo_base}/${z_ri_pkg_path}:${z_ri_tag}"
451	      z_ri_locator="${z_ri_anchor}"
452	      buc_log_args "Image slot ${z_ri_n} (anchored): ${z_ri_ref}"
453	    else
454	      z_ri_ref="${z_ri_origin}"
455	      buc_log_args "Image slot ${z_ri_n} (pass-through): ${z_ri_ref}"
456	    fi
457	    case "${z_ri_n}" in
458	      1) z_image_ref_1="${z_ri_ref}"; z_image_locator_1="${z_ri_locator}" ;;
459	      2) z_image_ref_2="${z_ri_ref}"; z_image_locator_2="${z_ri_locator}" ;;
460	      3) z_image_ref_3="${z_ri_ref}"; z_image_locator_3="${z_ri_locator}" ;;
461	    esac
462	  done
463	
464	  # Platform count detection
465	  local z_platform_count=0
466	  local z_remaining_count="${z_platforms}"
467	  local z_p_count=""
468	  while test -n "${z_remaining_count}"; do
469	    z_p_count="${z_remaining_count%%,*}"
470	    z_platform_count=$((z_platform_count + 1))
471	    test "${z_remaining_count}" != "${z_p_count}" || break
472	    z_remaining_count="${z_remaining_count#*,}"
473	  done
474	  buc_log_args "Vessel platforms: ${z_platform_count} (${z_platforms})"
475	
476	  buc_log_args 'Extract git state for substitutions'
477	  local -r z_stitch_git_commit_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}git_commit.txt"
478	  local -r z_stitch_git_branch_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}git_branch.txt"
479	  local -r z_stitch_git_repo_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}git_repo.txt"
480	
481	  jq -r '.commit' "${ZRBFC_GIT_INFO_FILE}" > "${z_stitch_git_commit_file}" \
482	    || buc_die "Failed to extract git commit from info file"
483	  jq -r '.branch' "${ZRBFC_GIT_INFO_FILE}" > "${z_stitch_git_branch_file}" \
484	    || buc_die "Failed to extract git branch from info file"
485	  jq -r '.repo'   "${ZRBFC_GIT_INFO_FILE}" > "${z_stitch_git_repo_file}" \
486	    || buc_die "Failed to extract git repo from info file"
487	
488	  local -r z_git_commit=$(<"${z_stitch_git_commit_file}")
489	  local -r z_git_branch=$(<"${z_stitch_git_branch_file}")
490	  local -r z_git_repo=$(<"${z_stitch_git_repo_file}")
491	
492	  test -n "${z_git_commit}" || buc_die "Git commit is empty"
493	  test -n "${z_git_branch}" || buc_die "Git branch is empty"
494	  test -n "${z_git_repo}"   || buc_die "Git repo is empty"
495	
496	  # Build strategy: compare vessel platforms against runner platform
497	  # If platforms exactly match the runner, no QEMU emulation is needed (native build).
498	  # Any difference (multi-platform or non-native single-platform) requires binfmt.
499	  local z_needs_binfmt="true"
500	  if test "${RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS// /,}" = "${RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM}"; then
501	    z_needs_binfmt="false"
502	  fi
503	
504	  local z_build_strategy=""
505	  if test "${z_needs_binfmt}" = "true"; then
506	    z_build_strategy="emulated multi-platform via QEMU (${RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS// /,})"
507	    buc_log_args "Build strategy: ${z_build_strategy} — rbgjb02 included"
508	  else
509	    z_build_strategy="native single-platform (${RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM})"
510	    buc_log_args "Build strategy: ${z_build_strategy} — rbgjb02 excluded"
511	  fi
512	
513	  # Step definitions: script|builder|entrypoint|id
514	  # Entrypoint 'bash' → #!/bin/bash shebang, 'sh' → #!/bin/sh shebang (GCB script field)
515	  # Delimiter is | because image refs contain colons (sha256 digests)
516	  # Pipeline: buildx --push → per-platform pullback → SLSA provenance via images: field
517	  local z_step_defs=(
518	    "rbgjb01-derive-tag-base.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_gcloud}|bash|derive-tag-base"
519	  )
520	  if test "${z_needs_binfmt}" = "true"; then
521	    z_step_defs+=("rbgjb02-qemu-binfmt.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|qemu-binfmt")
522	  fi
523	  z_step_defs+=(
524	    "rbgjb03-buildx-push-image.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|buildx-push-image"
525	    "rbgjb04-per-platform-pullback.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|per-platform-pullback"
526	    "rbgjb05-push-per-platform.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|push-per-platform"
527	    "rbgjb06-push-diags.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|push-diags"
528	  )
529	
530	  # Compute platform suffixes (used in images: field and substitutions)
531	  # Always computed: linux/amd64 → -amd64, linux/arm64 → -arm64, linux/arm/v7 → -armv7
532	  local z_platform_suffixes=""
533	  local z_platform_suffixes_csv=""
534	  local z_remaining_plats="${z_platforms}"
535	  local z_plat=""
536	  local z_suffix=""
537	  while test -n "${z_remaining_plats}"; do
538	    z_plat="${z_remaining_plats%%,*}"
539	    # Strip linux/ prefix, collapse remaining slashes: linux/arm/v7 → armv7
540	    z_suffix="${z_plat#linux/}"
541	    z_suffix="${z_suffix//\//}"
542	    z_suffix="-${z_suffix}"
543	    if test -n "${z_platform_suffixes}"; then
544	      z_platform_suffixes="${z_platform_suffixes},${z_suffix}"
545	    else
546	      z_platform_suffixes="${z_suffix}"
547	    fi
548	    test "${z_remaining_plats}" != "${z_plat}" || break
549	    z_remaining_plats="${z_remaining_plats#*,}"
550	  done
551	  z_platform_suffixes_csv="${z_platform_suffixes}"
552	  buc_log_args "Platform suffixes: ${z_platform_suffixes_csv}"
553	
554	  local z_def=""
555	  local z_script=""
556	  local z_builder=""
557	  local z_entrypoint=""
558	  local z_id=""
559	  local z_script_path=""
560	  local z_body=""
561	  local z_shebang=""
562	  local z_body_file=""
563	  local z_escaped_file=""
564	  local z_steps_file=""
565	  local z_accumulator_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}steps.json"
566	
567	  buc_log_args "Initializing empty steps array"
568	  echo "[]" > "${z_accumulator_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize steps JSON"
569	
570	  for z_def in "${z_step_defs[@]}"; do
571	    IFS='|' read -r z_script z_builder z_entrypoint z_id <<< "${z_def}"
572	    z_script_path="${ZRBFD_RBGJB_STEPS_DIR}/${z_script}"
573	    z_body_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}${z_id}_body.txt"
574	    z_escaped_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}${z_id}_escaped.txt"
575	    z_steps_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}${z_id}_steps.json"
576	
577	    test -f "${z_script_path}" || buc_die "Step script not found: ${z_script_path}"
578	
579	    buc_log_args "Reading script body for ${z_id} (skip shebang, comments pass through)"
580	    zrbfc_write_script_body "${z_script_path}" "${z_body_file}" || buc_die "Failed to read step script: ${z_script_path}"
581	    z_body=$(<"${z_body_file}")
582	    test -n "${z_body}" || buc_die "Empty script body: ${z_script_path}"
583	
584	    buc_log_args "Baking pinned image refs and build strategy into script text"
585	    z_body="${z_body//\$\{ZRBF_TOOL_BINFMT\}/${z_rbfc_tool_binfmt}}"
586	    z_body="${z_body//\$\{ZRBF_BUILD_STRATEGY\}/${z_build_strategy}}"
587	
588	    case "${z_entrypoint}" in
589	      bash) z_shebang="#!/bin/bash" ;;
590	      sh)   z_shebang="#!/bin/sh" ;;
591	      *)    buc_die "Unknown entrypoint: ${z_entrypoint}" ;;
592	    esac
593	    printf '%s\n%s' "${z_shebang}" "${z_body}" > "${z_escaped_file}" \
594	      || buc_die "Failed to write script body for ${z_id}"
595	
596	    buc_log_args "Appending step ${z_id} to JSON array"
597	    jq \
598	      --arg name "${z_builder}" \
599	      --arg id "${z_id}" \
600	      --arg dir "${z_sigil}" \
601	      --rawfile script "${z_escaped_file}" \
602	      '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, dir: $dir, script: $script}]' \
603	      "${z_accumulator_file}" > "${z_steps_file}" \
604	      || buc_die "Failed to append step ${z_id} to JSON"
605	    mv "${z_steps_file}" "${z_accumulator_file}" \
606	      || buc_die "Failed to update steps JSON for ${z_id}"
607	  done
608	
609	  # === Combined conjure: embed about steps after image steps ===
610	  # About steps use _RBGA_* substitutions delivered via the substitutions block.
611	  # _RBGA_BUILD_ID is the lone exception — Cloud Build job ID, only available
612	  # at runtime as the built-in $BUILD_ID env var; rewritten below.
613	
614	  buc_log_args "Assembling about steps for combined conjure"
615	  local -r z_about_steps_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}about_steps.json"
616	  zrbfc_assemble_about_steps "${z_about_steps_file}" "${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}about_"
617	
618	  # About steps run in vessel dir so .hallmark from rbgjb01 is accessible
619	  buc_log_args "Adding dir field to about steps for vessel directory ${z_sigil}"
620	  local -r z_about_with_dir="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}about_with_dir.json"
621	  jq --arg dir "${z_sigil}" '[.[] | . + {dir: $dir}]' \
622	    "${z_about_steps_file}" > "${z_about_with_dir}" \
623	    || buc_die "Failed to add dir to about steps"
624	
625	  # Build ID: $BUILD_ID → GCB built-in available as env var
626	  buc_log_args "Post-processing about steps: build ID from env"
627	  local -r z_about_processed="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}about_processed.json"
628	  local z_about_content
629	  z_about_content=$(<"${z_about_with_dir}") \
630	    || buc_die "Failed to read about steps for post-processing"
631	  z_about_content="${z_about_content//\$\{_RBGA_BUILD_ID:-\}/\$BUILD_ID}"
632	  printf '%s' "${z_about_content}" > "${z_about_processed}" \
633	    || buc_die "Failed to post-process about steps for conjure"
634	
635	  buc_log_args "Combining image steps and about steps"
636	  local -r z_combined_steps_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}combined_steps.json"
637	  jq -s '.[0] + .[1]' "${z_accumulator_file}" "${z_about_processed}" \
638	    > "${z_combined_steps_file}" || buc_die "Failed to combine image and about steps"
639	  z_accumulator_file="${z_combined_steps_file}"
640	
641	  # Fallback for -diags extraction failure; -diags is the primary path for conjure
642	  buc_log_args "Reading Dockerfile content for _RBGA_DOCKERFILE_CONTENT substitution"
643	  local z_stitch_dockerfile_content=""
644	  local -r z_stitch_df_max_bytes=4000
645	  if test -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE:-}"; then
646	    z_stitch_dockerfile_content=$(<"${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}")
647	    if test "${#z_stitch_dockerfile_content}" -gt "${z_stitch_df_max_bytes}"; then
648	      buc_warn "Dockerfile exceeds 4KB substitution limit (${#z_stitch_dockerfile_content} bytes) — recipe.txt via -diags only"
649	      z_stitch_dockerfile_content=""
650	    fi
651	  fi
652	
653	  # Compose builds.create Build resource
654	  # All values resolved directly — no placeholders, no post-processing jq surgery.
655	  # Context extraction step prepended; mason SA included; images: field uses inscribe_ts.
656	  buc_log_args "Composing builds.create Build resource"
657	  local -r z_build_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}build.json"
658	  local -r z_mason_sa="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}"
659	
660	  # Context extraction step (first step — extracts build context from pouch in GAR)
661	  local -r z_extract_step_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}extract_step.json"
662	  jq -n \
663	    --arg name "${z_rbfc_tool_docker}" \
664	    --arg ctx_tag "${z_context_tag}" \
665	    --arg sigil "${z_sigil}" \
666	    '{
667	      name: $name,
668	      id: "extract-context",
669	      entrypoint: "/bin/bash",
670	      args: ["-lc", ("set -euo pipefail\necho \"Extracting build context from GAR\"\nCONTAINER=$$(docker create " + $ctx_tag + " /nonexistent)\nmkdir -p /workspace/" + $sigil + "\ndocker cp $${CONTAINER}:/build-context/. /workspace/" + $sigil + "/\ndocker rm $${CONTAINER}\necho \"Context extracted:\"\nls -la /workspace/" + $sigil + "/")]
671	    }' > "${z_extract_step_file}" \
672	    || buc_die "Failed to compose context extraction step"
673	
674	  # Step 0: in-pool reliquary preflight (defense-in-depth)
675	  local -r z_preflight_step_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}preflight_step.json"
676	  zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step "${z_preflight_step_file}" "${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}"
677	
678	  # Combine: [preflight] + [extract-context] + image steps + about steps
679	  local -r z_all_steps_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}all_steps.json"
680	  jq -s '.[0] + .[1] + .[2]' \
681	    "${z_preflight_step_file}" \
682	    <(jq -s '.' "${z_extract_step_file}") \
683	    "${z_accumulator_file}" \
684	    > "${z_all_steps_file}" || buc_die "Failed to combine preflight, context extraction, and image+about steps"
685	
686	  # images: field — one per-platform attest tag per platform for SLSA provenance via CB images: push
687	  # These are durable provenance-carrying tags on the single attest package
688	  # (rbi_hm/<H>/attest); deleted only by abjure.
689	  local z_images_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}images.json"
690	  local z_attest_pkg="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/attest"
691	  local z_remaining_suffixes="${z_platform_suffixes_csv}"
692	  local z_img_suffix=""
693	  echo "[]" > "${z_images_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize images JSON"
694	  while test -n "${z_remaining_suffixes}"; do
695	    z_img_suffix="${z_remaining_suffixes%%,*}"
696	    jq --arg uri "${z_attest_pkg}:${z_hallmark}${z_img_suffix}" \
697	      '. + [$uri]' "${z_images_file}" > "${z_images_file}.tmp" \
698	      || buc_die "Failed to append image URI"
699	    mv "${z_images_file}.tmp" "${z_images_file}" \
700	      || buc_die "Failed to update images JSON"
701	    test "${z_remaining_suffixes}" != "${z_img_suffix}" || break
702	    z_remaining_suffixes="${z_remaining_suffixes#*,}"
703	  done
704	
705	  local -r z_cb_build_id='$BUILD_ID'
706	
707	  # Pool routing: conjure/bind use vessel's egress mode
708	  local z_conjure_pool=""
709	  case "${RBRV_EGRESS_MODE}" in
710	    rbnve_tether) z_conjure_pool="${RBDC_POOL_TETHER}" ;;
711	    rbnve_airgap) z_conjure_pool="${RBDC_POOL_AIRGAP}" ;;
712	    *) buc_die "Unknown RBRV_EGRESS_MODE: ${RBRV_EGRESS_MODE}" ;;
713	  esac
714	
715	  jq -n \
716	    --slurpfile zjq_steps  "${z_all_steps_file}" \
717	    --slurpfile zjq_images "${z_images_file}" \
718	    --arg zjq_dockerfile        "${z_dockerfile_name}" \
719	    --arg zjq_vessel            "${z_sigil}" \
720	    --arg zjq_platforms         "${z_platforms}" \
721	    --arg zjq_platform_suffixes "${z_platform_suffixes_csv}" \
722	    --arg zjq_gar_location      "${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}" \
723	    --arg zjq_gar_project       "${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}" \
724	    --arg zjq_gar_repository    "${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}" \
725	    --arg zjq_git_commit        "${z_git_commit}" \
726	    --arg zjq_git_branch        "${z_git_branch}" \
727	    --arg zjq_git_repo          "${z_git_repo}" \
728	    --arg zjq_gar_host_suffix   "${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}" \
729	    --arg zjq_hallmarks_root    "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}" \
730	    --arg zjq_hallmark          "${z_hallmark}" \

772	        _RBGY_IMAGE_2:             $zjq_image_2,

774	        _RBGY_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE:  $zjq_basename_image,
775	        _RBGY_ARK_BASENAME_ATTEST: $zjq_basename_attest,

779	        _RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT:      $zjq_hallmarks_root,

794	        _RBGR_GAR_PATH:            $zjq_rbga_gar_path,
795	        _RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT:    $zjq_reliquaries_root,
796	        _RBGR_RELIQUARY:           $zjq_reliquary,

815	  buc_log_args "Stitched ${#z_step_defs[@]} + context + about steps to ${z_output_path}"

818	
819	
820	# Push vessel build context to GAR as a FROM SCRATCH OCI image (the pouch).
821	# The pouch carries the Dockerfile and supporting files that GCB
822	# needs in /workspace during the build.
823	#
824	# Args: token  sigil  build_context_path  hallmark
825	# Side-effect: writes context image tag to ${ZRBFD_CONTEXT_PREFIX}tag.txt
826	zrbfd_push_build_context() {
827	  zrbfd_sentinel
828	
829	  local -r z_token="$1"
830	  local -r z_sigil="$2"
831	  local -r z_bldctx="$3"
832	  local -r z_hallmark="$4"
833	
834	  test -d "${z_bldctx}" || buc_die "Build context directory not found: ${z_bldctx}"
835	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Hallmark required for pouch tag"
836	
837	  local -r z_gar_host="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}"
838	  local -r z_context_tag_file="${ZRBFD_CONTEXT_PREFIX}tag.txt"
839	  local -r z_context_dockerfile="${ZRBFD_CONTEXT_PREFIX}Dockerfile"
840	
841	  local -r z_context_tag="${z_gar_host}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/pouch:${z_hallmark}"
842	
843	  # Build FROM SCRATCH image containing build context
844	  buc_step "Building context image for ${z_sigil}"
845	  printf 'FROM scratch\nCOPY . /build-context/\n' > "${z_context_dockerfile}" \
846	    || buc_die "Failed to write context Dockerfile"
847	
848	  # docker is Windows-native under Cygwin; hand it Windows-form paths. The
849	  # generated absolute -f is the proven failure; the context positional is
850	  # routed too because dockerfile-inside-context is not guaranteed (no-op when a
851	  # path is already relative or native, and off Cygwin).
852	  local z_norm_dockerfile=""
853	  z_norm_dockerfile=$(buc_native_path_capture "${z_context_dockerfile}") \
854	    || buc_die "Cannot normalize context Dockerfile path for docker: ${z_context_dockerfile}"
855	  local z_norm_context=""
856	  z_norm_context=$(buc_native_path_capture "${z_bldctx}") \
857	    || buc_die "Cannot normalize build-context path for docker: ${z_bldctx}"
858	
859	  docker build --platform "${RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM}" -f "${z_norm_dockerfile}" -t "${z_context_tag}" "${z_norm_context}" \
860	    || buc_die "Failed to build context image"
861	
862	  # Push to GAR
863	  buc_step "Pushing context image to GAR"
864	  rbgo_docker_login "${z_token}" "${z_gar_host}"
865	
866	  docker push "${z_context_tag}" \
867	    || buc_die "Failed to push context image to GAR"
868	
869	  echo "${z_context_tag}" > "${z_context_tag_file}" \
870	    || buc_die "Failed to persist context image tag"
871	
872	  buc_info "Context image pushed: ${z_context_tag}"
873	}
874	
875	
876	######################################################################
877	# External Functions (rbfd_*)
878	
879	# Derived-pull base-anchor election (bole kind). If the immediately-prior
880	# dispatch was a bole ensconce, it handed a touchmark forward through the depth-1
881	# chain; resolve it to a Lode locator and persist it as the conjure base ANCHOR
882	# before the build loads the vessel. This is the fetched-side "the vessel pulls
883	# the resolved coordinate from the capture-file": capture stays pure (writes no
884	# vessel config) and election writes it here, durably, so repeated conjures reuse
885	# the persisted ANCHOR without re-ensconcing.
886	#
887	# No buf_relay: the touchmark is consumed terminally. Relaying would forward a
888	# stale touchmark into a later unrelated ordain (cross-vessel leakage), so the
889	# depth-1 adjacency — ensconce immediately precedes the first ordain — is the
890	# deliberate contract, not a limitation to paper over. Absence is normal (no
891	# fresh capture in the chain): leave any existing ANCHOR untouched. A present
892	# touchmark elects only when its kind-brand is bole; a non-bole touchmark (a wsl
893	# underpin or reliquary conclave chained ahead of this ordain) likewise leaves
894	# the ANCHOR as-is. A bole presence overwrites, so a re-capture repoints.
895	# Args: rbrv_file
896	zrbfd_elect_base_anchor() {
897	  zrbfd_sentinel
898	
899	  local -r z_rbrv_file="${1:?Vessel regime file required}"
900	  test -f "${z_rbrv_file}" || buc_die "Vessel regime file not found: ${z_rbrv_file}"
901	
902	  test -f "${BURD_PREVIOUS_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK}" || {
903	    buc_log_args "No Lode touchmark in the chain — base ANCHOR left as-is"
904	    return 0
905	  }
906	
907	  local z_brand=""
908	  z_brand=$(buf_read_fact "${RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND}") || buc_die "Failed to read kind-brand chaining fact"
909	
910	  # Only a bole carries a base image to elect. A non-bole capture (an underpin or
911	  # conclave run earlier in the same depth-1 chain) hands its own touchmark
912	  # forward but has no base ANCHOR to give — leave the ANCHOR as-is. A brand
913	  # outside the authored enum is corruption.
914	  local z_tag=""
915	  case "${z_brand}" in
916	    "${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_BOLE}")
917	      z_tag="${RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE}" ;;
